text: Today's video is about AsyncIO How we can get asynchronous tasks done from python and how it will save us a lot of time Let's go to the computer screen and let's get started To understand AsyncIO we have to understand that AsyncIO is neither multi-threading nor multi-processing It is just a way to run your code concurrently What is a typical execution flow in a python program? Let's say you have a python program Let's say I have a function 1 which I am running and after this I have a function 2 which I am running and function 3 which I am running Now what will happen is when function 1 will be completed then function 2 will run and then function 3 will run This is known by the world So if I import you by time and then import function 1 and then time.sleep Let's say I do 3 seconds and then I print func1 and then I make func2 and func3 So that time will be wasted Because I know that you all know that by doing this first function 1 will execute then function 2 will execute then function 3 will execute as you can see here First of all, it will execute func1 and then func2 and then time.sleep and then function 3 So this is a typical execution flow which we all know But sometimes it can happen that you want to get some data from the network Let's say you want to download an image Now while image 1 is downloading Can't we download image 2 at the same time? Technically, we can But due to this Python's shortcoming, you can't do this So async programming is used for this It is used for such use cases If I make any function asynchronous function So what will happen It will become an async function And now I can await it Now as I run it You can see it is giving me an error It is saying that you have never awaited it So what I can do I can say async, def, main I can make a main function And I can await all three of them So they will run one by one But what did it change? Nothing In main, all three were running one by one Now they will run one by one So await means that you wait for any async function to finish Then run the next function Then run the next function Now how can I concurrently run I can run it concurrently So to run it, what can we do We can say asyncio.run And then main as a function So I think I didn't import asyncio No problem So now you can see I have imported asyncio asyncio And if I run it Then main function will run Now you will say that nothing has changed If we want to change things How to change We will change it by making a task We will say asyncio.createTask Now what does createTask do CreateTask makes a task Which will be executed whenever I will show you, as I wrote function1 Now I have made task asyncio.createTask And This task will run whenever time is available Now what does it mean, I will show you I will do one thing here I have put time.sleep of 3 I will await it and put asyncio.sleep And put only 1 second sleep So I will put 1 second sleep here And here I will put 4 seconds sleep So now if I show you After running it So our function1 is scheduled Function2 is running When function2 was running I got time to run function1 So by doing this Whenever it will get time, it will run But here we don't get a good organization of things That's why we use gather syntax Now what is gather syntax, I will tell you If I do something like this I will say that I want to await asyncio.gather And I will these 3 functions Which are my function1, function2, function3 I will show you Here I will do function1 or function2 Or function3 So by doing this All three will be executed And if I print it So their values When all three will run, I will get So you see here All three functions are running And here they are not returning anything So I am getting none So if I return something here Let's say I return Let's say function1 Returns Harry So here all three will run First function1, then function2 In fact all three are running parallelly But function3 is taking more time Because it is taking 4 seconds sleep And after that, Harry, none and none Now you will say These are very boring functions Function1, function2 and function3 Do something and show So if I do something and show you So I will say request python Download a file So how can a file be downloaded So this is the request module So this is its code So let me do one thing Let me try out this code And what I will do here What is it doing Downloading the fab icon of Instagram Basically, it is not doing anything too long or wide So if I give it a little space By doing this So you see what it is doing This is the URL, this is request.get Then by opening it, it is writing it The response content And I'm going to remove this sleep from here And I print it Function1 And along with that, I copy it I put it here too And I put it here too And here I am Instagram2 and here I am I will save it in the name of Instagram3 Now see as soon as I run it Then you see All three icons are downloaded together You saw this It is downloaded parallelly If I had not done this, I would have run it like this If I had run it like this I mean I would not have done this And I would have done it like this I will show you how I would have done it Yes, I would have done it like this And if I had done it like this I would have run it like this That they are running one by one And I will definitely delete them Because they are async functions Basically, I am saying that run them one by one And I delete it You saw how fast Instagram Instagram1 and
